context: Tests whether the agent implements the WooCommerce maintenance plugin using Action Scheduler for scheduling, applies the correct expiry thresholds and LIMIT sizes for each DELETE operation, and uses a two-step approach for transient cleanup.

checklist.name: Action Scheduler used
checklist.description: Plugin uses as_schedule_recurring_action() (not wp_schedule_event) to register the daily cleanup

checklist.name: No double-registration guard
checklist.description: Uses as_next_scheduled_action() to check before calling as_schedule_recurring_action() (prevents duplicate schedule entries on re-activation)

checklist.name: Session expiry threshold is 48 hours
checklist.description: The session DELETE query uses a threshold of 48 hours (time() - 48 * HOUR_IN_SECONDS or equivalent) not any other duration

checklist.name: Session DELETE uses LIMIT 5000
checklist.description: The DELETE query for woocommerce_sessions includes LIMIT 5000 (not unlimited, not a different limit)

checklist.name: Transient timeout step
checklist.description: First transient DELETE targets option_name LIKE '_transient_timeout_%' with option_value < UNIX_TIMESTAMP() and includes LIMIT 5000

checklist.name: Orphaned transient step
checklist.description: Second transient DELETE removes orphaned _transient_ entries that have no matching _transient_timeout_ row (via LEFT JOIN or subquery), with LIMIT 5000

checklist.name: Log rotation interval is 30 days
checklist.description: The woocommerce_log DELETE query uses an interval of 30 days (DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY) or equivalent)

checklist.name: Log DELETE uses LIMIT 10000
checklist.description: The DELETE query for woocommerce_log includes LIMIT 10000

checklist.name: Cleanup scheduled at off-peak
checklist.description: The recurring schedule is set to run at a low-traffic time (e.g., midnight / 'tomorrow midnight' or another off-peak offset) rather than immediately or at an arbitrary offset

checklist.name: Batch size rationale documented
checklist.description: cleanup-design.md explains that LIMIT values are chosen to avoid long table locks or equivalent explanation for batching
